reviews.review_title: Decent App to Get You Moving
reviews.review_description:
Good app if you want to get  some sport specific exercises and you want a fantasy theme (kind of).  There is little in game training and weak tutorials so if you are doing something wrong, you will never know.  

On the plus side, there are a wide range of different exercises and most of them are good.  

On the negative side, there is no instruction on what the app is looking for with some exercises and the verbal coaching is rare and terrible.  The assistant might be great if you are 10 but otherwise he is just annoying.  There are a number of different worlds you must complete but they are all basically a reskin of the same world.  My final complaint is there is no time listed before starting a level so it could be between 9 and 15 minutes - I usually like to do a couple before getting ready for work and you have no idea how much time that will take.

This app is so much fun! I appreciate the details and variety of exercises, which are different from the competition and really give a full body exhausting workout. The graphics and music are very well done. If you are interested in a great fitness game, check this out. It should be listed as a fitness game as well as action so customers browsing the fitness category will find this title. I'm also happy there is a mixed reality mode. Level 22 now and no saving issues.

reviews.review_title: Bought it for my kids, but it kicks my b
reviews.review_description:
I bought this to give my kids a way to get exercise while having fun. I tried it, and omg, its a butt kicker of a workout. I did the whole first level, including the boss fight with the weird tree, and my shoulders were sore for days, I was drenched in sweat, and definitely got a good workout. 

Don't let the kiddie graphics and kiddie storyline fool you, adults can definitely get a good workout from this game. I now incorporate this game in my own exercise routine in addition to my kids using it. 

The only reason I gave it 4 stars instead of 5 is that there isn't really much variety once you've done all the different movements. They just keep getting recycled in different orders throughout the levels. But if you are willing to look past that, its a great fitness game suitable for all ages.

deleted my progress, which is the only thing pushing you to go further.  as mentioned in other reviews, the rest is just recycled and the exercises mostly don't feel like actual gameplay; its almost just like doing exercises for points.  the devs also didn't respond to my message about the problem.  while I'm beyond my return period, meta seemed willing to just refund me given the functionality break.  i plan to take them up on that if i don't get resolution soon some other way.
Was really looking forward to enjoying but it won't pick up my hand movement after multiple resets and recalibrations. 😞

reviews.review_title: Very disappointed to lose all my progre
reviews.review_description: I really enjoyed this game, but after not playing for a couple of months, all my progress was gone. I had invested a lot of time, and losing everything without any explanation was really disappointing. I reached out to the developers, but I haven’t received any reply. It’s very frustrating and discouraging to lose all that progress after spending so much time playing.

Initially I would have given this 3.5 stars. It is a bit boring and redundant but a great workout that makes you sweat, so I wanted to keep using it. However when I went in last, all my progress is lost. Seems they've closed up shop? I wish you could keep your progress on your headset then instead of their servers. I have tried reaching out to them and heard nothing in response. :( I paid for this app, so this is not okay. If it were free, sure. Even if they've bailed on it, we should be left with a useable experience.

Really fun and a good work out would have give five stars but after months if playing and completing I have just logged in and lost all my progress I hope the devs read this and respond as it’s frustrating

One of the few exercise games that actually do a good job of incorporating various different types of exercise into a single workout; and also feels like more of a "game" than just your normal workout which is great.
Only real issue with it is activities which require a bit of accuracy (tennis/archery), as aiming properly in VR can be jank at times. Sucks to lose a streak or miss a collectable due to the aiming being a complete mess.

Should also mention I did have the save bug which other reviews have also mentioned, but it got fixed manually by the devs after a week. Great that it got fixed, bad that saves appear to be server-side and this can happen at all. Why is single player game got server-side saves I have no idea.

reviews.review_title: Happy to be back at it
reviews.review_description:
EDIT: The devs restored my progress and the game is working again. I’m really happy to be back at it again because this is a decent workout. It’s a good one to have in your exercise repertoire.

ORIGINAL: I do actually really enjoy this game and it’s one of my go-to fitness apps. But I’ve suddenly lost all my progress and it’s reset me to Level One. Theres no way I’m starting again from the start - is there a fix to this?

developer_response.body:
We’re really sorry to hear about the progress loss — we know how discouraging that can feel after all your hard work. Please reach out to our support team at fitnessfables.support@immersion.pl
 so we can look into your account and help restore your progress. We’d love to get you back on track with your adventure!

reviews.review_description:
Changing to a 4 star. I still like this game

PROs:
* Move Heaven: has targeted so many muscle group, I am hurting (in a good way). I almost feel like I don't need another game, there's dodging, swinging, boxing, squating, so much more, and it's exciting. 

* Very addictive: once I was done with the tutorial level and my settings were configure, I couldn't stop. 

CONs:

* Does not "Show, not tell" with story-telling: i was a bit disappointed by the game's need to explain the story rather than show, as it was a bit off-putting. I don't care for this world, I just got here, give me a reason to save this world. 

* Tutorial immediately assumes player is Right Dominant:  The tutorial didn't immediately ask if the player is left or right handed, as it encourages the player to PLAY the game immediately instead of addressing some of the more important settings found in the setting. As a result, I went into the tutorial stage with the controls set as someone who is right dominant, forcing the tennis section to only select the right hand, not allowing for any switching. I had to exit the tutorial, go to the settings, and then miss the rest of the tutorial because I thought I could hold A to skip one part of the tutorial, not all of it. So I would suggest to give a Professor Oak style of asking the player what their preference is, or at least make the paddle hand become ambidextrous.

* Bow & Arrow with the scope on right dominant side on left handed mode: This was silly, but when attempting to use the Bow & Arrow in a left dominant side, the scope is only located on the left side, meaning I had no practical use for it. So the bow is still in the same stance as before.   

Everything else is great.

They probably forgot to pay their server bill because the game now erases all progress every time you quit the game. The worst thing is that I'm only interested in the tennis training level, but you can't even initiate that without beating three levels and a boss. Now I have to do that every time I want to work out? No thanks. This app is absolutely worthless to me now.

developer_response.body:
We’re really sorry to hear about the progress loss — we know how discouraging that can feel after all your hard work. Please reach out to our support team at fitnessfables.support@immersion.pl
 so we can look into your account and help restore your progress. We’d love to get you back on track with your adventure!

reviews.review_description: Like others on here after having a break I lost all my progress which had taken ages to achieve. Very de motivating

It's a chance after months or days to lose all your progress just before the last level like me. I'm very disappointed. And there is no way to choose freely any level I want. I don't like to start from the beginning.

developer_response.body:
We’re really sorry to hear about the progress loss — we know how discouraging that can feel after all your hard work. Please reach out to our support team at fitnessfables.support@immersion.pl
 so we can look into your account and help restore your progress. We’d love to get you back on track with your adventure!

reviews.review_title: Best Fitness Game On The Store!
reviews.review_description: My progress wiped recently too. They restored it very quickly after I emailed them - within a day! I have been using this game to help me gain some fitness, and I’ve really enjoyed the experience. I still play it even after completing all the levels, it’s just that good! Highly recommend. Disability friendly too! I have very poor mobility that notably improved thanks to this game.

I had not gone in for a few days. I went on yesterday and it started me back at the beginning. I quit and went back in because I thought something was wrong. Then it asked to create a new profile. So disheartening after all of the hard work. The game would have received 5 stars but they lost that review.

developer_response.body:
We’re really sorry to hear about the progress loss — we know how discouraging that can feel after all your hard work. Please reach out to our support team at fitnessfables.support@immersion.pl
 so we can look into your account and help restore your progress. We’d love to get you back on track with your adventure!

reviews.review_description: I played the game for more than a half year but lost all my progress in a day. Please fix my profile!

reviews.review_title: Love the game but suddenly lost progress
reviews.review_description:
I’ve been enjoying the game and initially gave it 5 stars.  I’m not crazy about the somewhat childlike story but I love the activities.  They feel very realistic.

Today I removed a star because my progress was somehow completely lost and I cannot find any way to get it reset.

Progress saves again.  This is my favorite workout so put back the star.  I hope the progress sticks this time but for three days it has.  /fingers crossed

developer_response.body:
We’re really sorry to hear about the progress loss — we know how discouraging that can feel after all your hard work. Please reach out to our support team at fitnessfables.support@immersion.pl
 so we can look into your account and help restore your progress. We’d love to get you back on track with your adventure!

reviews.review_description:
I like playing but some of the exercises need to be improved.  Especially the power wheel. First two times in the level it works fine.  Then no matter how fast or slow you spin your arms, you don’t go anywhere.  No way to skip the exercise.  No way to finish the level. Would like to see it updated and bugs fixed!


Well, down to 1 star!  Have done what developers suggest. It’s been 6 months and still nothing.   Would be good if it worked!

Fitness Fables is a fun workout game, but I’m starting to get a little frustrated with a couple of things. Battle ropes scoring feels off,  I’m hitting targets and getting points, yet I always drop in rank. Is there some trick or technique I’m missing? Boxing also needs work since punches don’t register. Aside from those issues, it’s a great game that really gets you moving.

They restored my lost progress quickly for those  who log on and find their progress gone.  So don't freak out.

Lots of variety with the different exercises, lots of squats, so it's good for abs, and it plays like a game, so you want to keep playing to the end.  

Just play this consistently, and it's the only VR workout program you'll need.

I enjoyed this app and I have tried Supernatural to Pilates in Meta. I wish I could remove some exercises like the bike for arms. I'm still recovering from a shoulder injury and this one hurt after a few rotations. Also for people who can't squat deep it would be helpful to raise up the squat height. Also would be great to be able to have different music in the background. I'll continue this app and hopefully it's one I can stick with.

Good with a ranking system that encourage progress.
Edit: Scores deleted no news from the company.

developer_response.body:
We’re really sorry to hear about the progress loss — we know how discouraging that can feel after all your hard work. Please reach out to our support team at fitnessfables.support@immersion.pl
 so we can look into your account and help restore your progress. We’d love to get you back on track with your adventure!

reviews.review_description: Like others, I lost all my progress after months of using the app. So I'm very disappointed about that. It's a great app otherwise.

developer_response.body:
We’re really sorry to hear about the progress loss — we know how discouraging that can feel after all your hard work. Please reach out to our support team at fitnessfables.support@immersion.pl
 so we can look into your account and help restore your progress. We’d love to get you back on track with your adventure!

reviews.review_description: How did this happen and will it happen again? I play many games and never been reseted before. Please give us the reason for trusting this app again.

developer_response.body:
We’re really sorry to hear about the progress loss — we know how discouraging that can feel after all your hard work. Please reach out to our support team at fitnessfables.support@immersion.pl
 so we can look into your account and help restore your progress. We’d love to get you back on track with your adventure!

reviews.review_description: Love the game, but got on tonight and was kicked back to the beginning. Hopefully they will see the reviews and resolve the issue.

developer_response.body:
We’re really sorry to hear about the progress loss — we know how discouraging that can feel after all your hard work. Please reach out to our support team at fitnessfables.support@immersion.pl
 so we can look into your account and help restore your progress. We’d love to get you back on track with your adventure!

reviews.review_description: I have tried almost all the stand-alone fitness apps (no subscription) and this app is one of the best. It incorporates some activities such as paddling  and rope swing, which I haven't seen in other apps. I still think Les Mills is the best stand-alone app in the Quest store, but this one comes very close - one thing I would love is if it has hand-tracking and allows us to use weights or resistance bands when doing exercises such as biceps curl and sky press. The boxing sequence gets monotonous and doesn't register sometimes. The boss levels are definitely tiring but can get a bit repetitive. The blue host is a bit childish but not a deal breaker. The points system and leaderboard could be improved to motivate us better. I hope the developer keeps working on this to make it the number one fitness app!

I would love to play this game more, but it requires a stable Internet connection, which can be a challenge in my area where the Internet connection isn't very reliable, It would be nice if you had an offline mode, so I could play the game without worrying about an internet connection.

reviews.review_title: Fitness app first game play second
reviews.review_description: This game is definitely a fitness app first. I was hoping I would get more immersed in the gameplay as opposed to feeling like I'm doing a workout class.  Still a great game, but doesn't have that immersive "I don't even realize I am working out feeling."

reviews.review_title: Add adjustments for tall people please.
reviews.review_description: After the tutorial I was excited to see they asked for my height (which is 6’2) because all the contraptions seems very low. Sadly nothing visually or in gameplay changed after entering my height. Any action that required squatting down was never enough for the system. The shield and racket games I couldn’t get low enough for it to register. The skiing also was near impossible as it wanted me to go further than I could. Please add adjustments for tall people where getting down so low isn’t a requirement.
and there is no way to report bugs except using ratings....

developer_response.body:
We’re really sorry to hear about the progress loss — we know how discouraging that can feel after all your hard work. Please reach out to our support team at fitnessfables.support@immersion.pl
 so we can look into your account and help restore your progress. We’d love to get you back on track with your adventure!

reviews.review_description:
This is working really well for me because I need a distraction from the fact I'm working out haha!
Lost progress but developer reached out to fix it. Will restore 5 star when it's fixed because it's really a great app.

Don't worry, if you have lost progress they will fix it. This game is good and worth it!

developer_response.body:
We’re really sorry to hear about the progress loss — we know how discouraging that can feel after all your hard work. Please reach out to our support team at fitnessfables.support@immersion.pl
 so we can look into your account and help restore your progress. We’d love to get you back on track with your adventure!

reviews.review_title: Great game!  Could use some tweeks.
reviews.review_description: First I would like to say that this game is fantastic. I've only done the first levels up to the boss but it has you moving in several different ways and you're moving continuously. And it is whole body movement. There is a story to keep you involved. You will sweat and have fun doing it. I like the fact that it keeps you involved to keep you motivated. I will say I believe especially on the tennis part that there should be a way to adjust the racket as it's almost impossible to hit the targets. I have a full VR room setup and you use the entire room so you will need some space to play. I hope the davs are still involved and keep updating and hopefully adding some tweets to make this game perfect.
Really fun game. But when I finished the game it glitches and made me restart the whole game. Very fun and what a workout.

It's a good game that leaves me really sweaty but the number one issue i have is the rope swing, every single time i drop from 1st to last even though i'm hitting every orb at max power, i don't understand what i could be doing wrong? I saw someone else say the same in a review and the developer responded but didn't address why. Also the hit detection in the boxing is pretty bad, i don't like the ones where you have to hit two at the same time above your head, doesn't register half the time. And a proper leaderboard would be nice rather than just a few other players scores that you can only view while playing

reviews.review_title: Great work out, maybe too kiddie though
reviews.review_description: I really enjoyed the workout this game gave me. I think the main flame character is really annoying, and many of the sound effects of monsters is kind of cringeworthy, but overall the game is both fun and effective, and totally worth the money.

First of all, I really appreciate this game.  I bought Meta Quest 3 over three years ago specifically for fitness, so any game that can truly give me a sweat I appreciate.  I really like the variety of workouts.  I like that you are able to choose which exercises you want to do on the boss levels.  

Now for my many suggestions, as this game really needs improvements.  It has a lot of potential but the developers need to listen to the market feedback and put effort into updating the game: 

The host character (a blue flame) has the most annoying voice.  Is this marketed to 6-8 year old girls?  I assume this is for adults to workout, but I feel embarrassed by the blue flame host character.  I tend to skip her narration, and I’ve even turned her voice completely off in the audio settings.  The story is extremely uninspiring to me, personally.  I almost wish there wasn’t a “story.”  It feels trite and condescending.  

Some of the exercises work better than others.  For instance, the paddling and x-country skiing work well, but the squats, and many of the unique boss exercises are finnicky.   And the “shield” squat exercises for the boss are very confusing.  Hold for 3 seconds?  Hold what?  Keep squatting or just stay squatting?

There is very little explanation of how the points are calculated.  I will be far ahead in the leader boards to start out and then 20 seconds into battle ropes (for example) I will have dropped way down the list even without missing one target.  Doesn’t make sense to me at all.  Do the orbs even give you extra points, or are they just to unlock the extra levels?  

The orbs should really not be on the opposite of the river while kayaking on one side.  

There need to be a LOT more settings, including many more accessibility settings.  Some people may need to sit in the exercise and cannot squat, for example.  Or, you should be able to turn “off” some of the exercises you can’t do.  You should also be able to turn off the leader boards.  It might be motivating for some people, but other people may feel discouraged if they are on the bottom half and it will demotivate them.  

To me, it’s very annoying that only some exercises are including in “training” and some of them are lumped in with other exercises you may not want to do.  You should be able to do ALL of the exercises in training including the boss exercises.  Why can’t I practice rock climbing, ball throwing or battle drums?  

The music is annoying as hell.  It goes on and on and on and on.  In the first world, the drumming music didn’t even really match the beat of most of the exercises (like battle ropes) which made it difficult and confusing to keep a good rhythm.  I turned off the music completely.  

Collecting orbs to open extra levels that (are by the way very tedious) is not motivating.  By collecting orbs you should either get extra points, or maybe unlock new standalone exercises in the training section, or be able to customize your clothes or ‘board’ or something fun like that.  

The game feels like it’s in beta/early access.  It needs a lot of overhauling and additions made (see above).  But I think it’s on the right track.  It certainly mixes up my workout routine and gets me sweating, so I appreciate that.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your detailed review and feedback! We truly appreciate your thoughtful insights and are really happy to hear that you’re enjoying the variety of workouts and the sweat-inducing experience. 💪 We're also glad to hear that the game has potential to enhance your fitness routine!

We understand your concerns and frustrations, especially regarding the host character’s voice, the story, and certain exercises not working as smoothly as they should. We're listening, and we’ll take your suggestions into account for future updates. We definitely agree that a better balance between fun and functionality is important, and we’ll work on improving the clarity of the instructions for exercises like the “shield squat” and provide better explanations for the point system and leaderboard mechanics. 📊

As for the accessibility settings, we hear you loud and clear! We’re always looking to make the game more inclusive, so your suggestions on being able to modify or skip certain exercises, and the option to disable leaderboards, will be considered.

Regarding the orbs, music, and other gameplay aspects, your feedback is invaluable for refining the overall experience. We want to make sure every player feels motivated and engaged, so we’ll definitely review how to make the collection system and music more aligned with the flow of the game. 🎶

We’re already working on an update to improve these areas and more! Thank you again for taking the time to provide such comprehensive feedback. We truly appreciate your support, and we’ll keep working to make this game better with each update! If you have any further suggestions or questions, don’t hesitate to reach out to us at fitnessfables.support@immersion.pl. 🚀

reviews.review_title: Decent exercise game but still some bugs
reviews.review_description: Like the overall setup of the game, and it does keep me coming back to exercise almost daily. However it has a tendency to lose track of the "floor" meaning that sometimes a level will start thinking the floor is at your waist and make it difficult to reach the grips. Other times it seems to "drift" up over a level - particularly a problem with the rope swings where you often end up with the roof clipping your vision and just have to reach out and guess where the next rope is. Sometimes if you move quickly, it loses track of one hand or the other requiring you to have to stop and grab in mid-level. I am playing this on a Quest 3 and wonder if the issue is that this is tuned to the 2/3s instead.

reviews.review_title: Innovative Approach to Fitness bux fixed
reviews.review_description:
Update: After the last update, the skip for exercises works in forests but not against bos. PLEASE SOLVE THE PROBLEM. Problem is fixed set room scarpe. 

Fitness Fables introduces a unique and engaging way to work out by turning exercises into in-game challenges. Each movement corresponds to a specific piece of gym equipment, which helps users understand the real-world application of their training. I particularly appreciate how the game visually connects movements to actual exercises, making it easier to grasp their purpose and function.

Areas for Improvement

Some exercises are bugged even when performed correctly. For example, the one where you have to grab the handles and rotate your arms simultaneously doesn't register movements properly, even when done correctly. The same issue occurs with the squat using the detonator.

The exercises should be more varied.

Another issue is the excessive number of squats. While lower body workouts are essential, the current balance feels too squat-heavy, potentially leading to knee strain. Adding more variety to leg exercises would make the gameplay more balanced and safer for long-term use.

Suggestions for Enhancement
A key improvement would be the ability to skip or substitute exercises. This would allow players to customize their workouts based on their needs and limitations.

Additionally, I strongly recommend introducing a seated mode with tailored exercises and an adapted adventure. This would make the game more inclusive for users who prefer or need to train while seated, ensuring a full-body workout experience for everyone.

Implement these changes, and I’ll gladly give Fitness Fables a 5-star rating!

One of the best fitness apps available on Quest 3.  
The graphics are really nice, and it's a great adventure!  
I haven't sweated that much since Les Mills...  
My only regret is not buying it sooner 😂.  
Don't make the same mistake I did—buy it right away!

reviews.review_title: Lots of potential, but not polished
reviews.review_description:
I really love the variety of sports you can do with the app. Unlike many other apps focusing on boxing and squaring, there are actions for shoulders available as well.

However, not all actions are polished at the same level. From my perspective, tennis is the worst. I don’t like the idea of training single hand only, and it’s quite difficult to hit the ball coming from the weak side. The recent update enables the “accessibility” mode to skip certain exercises, but what I want is having a predefined list of exercises to be executed from the training, then the app can replace them with other exercises.

Also, the recent update claims improvements of the battle rope exercise, which I had no problem at all. After the update, I feel it’s difficult to hit the target at the right time…

reviews.review_title: Concept seems fun but very buggy
reviews.review_description: Game is simple, alittle too repetitive and child like for an adult.  Very buggy for me however as I havent been able to get past a load screen a couple times, often meaning I have to delete and reload it.  Too bad I bought the game too long ago for a refund though and I've barely played it.
Update: after one month I was traveling so I didn't play, all my progress gone 😭 which is very disappointing for a very good game like this

Update: thank you for listening to feedback and updating the game it is great fun! 

Great fun game, developers have done a good job with this! Tough on hard difficulty which is good! Couple of improvements could be made to boxing as its a little hit and miss whether you hit the targets but nice job so far.

Another fitness game I bought and will probably never play.
I don't understand all of the praise and five star reviews.
I really wanted to find a new exciting game to break up the VR exercise rut I'm in (Pistol Whip, Synthrider and Until You Fall) but once again it falls flat for me.
The exercises might work if the world and story were better but who is the target audience? Ten year old girls?  As an older adult I feel quite ridiculous with a glittering flying smurf fluttering about telling me I have to save the planet with magic and that the monster isn't really evil but only misunderstood.
And don't get me started on the canned looping music.
Overall it feels like the intended audience is for preschoolers and not adults.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your honest feedback. We understand that the style and theme of the game might not resonate with everyone, especially if you’re looking for something more mature. 🎮 We’re always aiming to create a fun and engaging experience for a wide audience, and we appreciate your thoughts on the world and story.

We’ll certainly keep your feedback in mind as we continue developing and refining the game. 🎶 As for the music, we’ve heard similar comments and will explore options for more variety in the future. Thanks for giving it a try, and we hope you find the right game that fits your preferences! 🙌

reviews.review_description:
The game overall feels great from what I've played and is definitely great cardio, almost feeling/ playing like a gamified version of Les Mills Bodycombat at times.
Graphics are clear, colorful, and with the unoffensive music,  it's all a very nice experience. I can easily see myself getting in plenty of cardio unknowingly by placing through the "levels" and trying to get the collectables.
The one reason I'm rating the game low is due to the absolutely unnecessary mistake of making a game VERY EASILY played solo made with unnecessary forced "Internet Connection Required". I'm sick of this practice and it definitely puts a bad spin on the title. 

Outside of the required unnecessary internet connection issue, I do still recommend the title!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback! We’re so glad to hear you’re enjoying the game and getting a great cardio workout from it. The comparison to Bodycombat is fantastic! 🎮💪

We totally understand your frustration with the "Internet Connection Required" message. The reason we need an internet connection is that all user progress, including your achievements and stats, are stored on our servers. This ensures your progress is safely saved and can be accessed across different devices, providing a seamless experience. 🌐💾

We’re actively looking at ways to improve this feature, and we appreciate your patience as we continue to work on updates. Thanks again for your support and your valuable input! If you have any more thoughts or suggestions, don’t hesitate to let us know!

reviews.review_description: this is a fun, creative take on arcade fitness. There are some nice accessibility features. However, if the developer is reading this, would you consider an option to have grip either toggle or hold? If someone has arthritis in their hand as I do, it is much more difficult to hold the grip for a long period of time as opposed to just toggling it once and then being able to not have to squeeze to hold. Would that be possible? Thanks for your consideration of this. ￼ I would reconsider my rating upwards, if the future becomes available ￼￼. thank you for getting back to me and informing me about the adaption already being available, sorry that I missed it! I have upped my rating to reflect the accessibility feature. ￼

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback! We're glad you're enjoying the game and its accessibility features. There is already an option to change the grip setting in the lobby settings under 'Interaction Mode.' You can adjust it there to better suit your needs. We appreciate your support and hope this improves your experience!

reviews.review_description:
It’s really great! Highly recommend. I find it’s great for burning more calories than other games like for example i love les mills workout thing but this distracts my mind more from the fact I’m doing exercise and Im pull into thinking I need to save these little worlds and characters which is really sweet.

Haven’t found any other game that the full body variety of exercise with the story to keep you motivated.

I hope the developers keep building on the game and adding more and more it it.

Definitely could be still better but the concept is so so solid. Please add more things and listen to the other reviews as I agree with suggested improvement.   

Helped me burn 2,000 calories a day at times so literally was pushing so hard and doing 5 hours a day and this app made thst possible.

Thank you.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the amazing feedback! We're so glad to hear that the game has helped you stay motivated and burn those calories! Your dedication is inspiring! 🙌🔥

We’re definitely committed to improving and expanding the game even further, and we truly appreciate your suggestions and support. We're taking all feedback into consideration as we continue to develop new features and content. Stay tuned for more updates, and we’ll keep working hard to make the experience even better! Thanks again for your kind words and for being a part of the Fitness Fables community! 💪✨

reviews.review_description:
So let me get this straight, you made a high score focused fitness game with some of the worst collision detection i have ever seen in VR and you thought that was a good idea? 

Precision is the name of the game in quite a few different situations in this game and in every one of them the game falls flat on its face which ruins the experience as a whole. 

1/5

Refunded

developer_response.body: Hey Kindred209, sorry to hear the game didn’t meet your expectations. We’re continuously working on improving collision detection and overall precision to make the experience smoother. We appreciate the feedback and will use it to refine future updates. If you have any specific details on where you ran into issues, feel free to reach out to fitnessfables.support@immersion.pl. Thanks for giving it a try!

reviews.review_description: Been playing for around 20 minutes but I can already comment that this game is WAY better than the over-hyped Shardfall in every way. Better storyline, better graphics, better music, loads of different movements and exercises and it's just more fun!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your fantastic review! 😊 We're thrilled to hear you're enjoying the game and that it stands out for you in so many ways. We put a lot of love into the story, graphics, and gameplay, so it’s awesome to know it’s paying off. Keep having fun, and we can’t wait to hear more about your journey! 💪🔥🎮

reviews.review_title: The best fitness game so far!
reviews.review_description: I have many VR games to keep fit. 2 table tennis and 2 boxing games, Beat Saber, tennis, etc. But none of them even come close to Fitness Fables. If you seriously want to lose weight and save a fortune on a personal trainer, this game will definitely meet your expectations. I have never sweated so much in my life! The variety of these exercises distinguishes it from other games where you basically do similar movements all the time. Here you have everything. It works your arms, legs, abs, back and even biceps!! Sometimes I put half a kilogram weights on my wrists and then I can barely finish the mission. After 30 minutes of playing you feel like you have had a real, solid workout. Immersion Games, you did a great job!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the amazing feedback! We're thrilled to hear that Fitness Fables is helping you achieve your fitness goals and providing such a solid workout experience. We’re always striving to bring variety and intensity to our workouts to keep things fresh and engaging. Your support means a lot, and we’ll continue working hard to improve and add more content. Keep up the great work and stay motivated! 💪🎮🔥

reviews.review_description: I've been doing this for days,  level 1.  There are no directions on what to do to get the gage to move our the sled to move forward.   No matter how fast I spin my arms, the movement doesn't register.  It shows burning calories and time,  but no progress on the level.  Very frustrating.

developer_response.body: It sounds like you're having a frustrating experience! A key tip is to make sure you've grabbed the exercise machine first—this is essential for registering movement and making progress in the level. Give that a try, and hopefully, you'll see the gauge move! Let us know if you need further help. 💪

reviews.review_description: This game is great fun but, unfortunately I have a rotator cuff injury in my left shoulder which is aggravated by the paddle wheel exercise where you have to rotate both arms in sync. Any chance of a future update that enables users to disable specific exercises?

The guide’s voice was irritating. The exercises are repetitive and boring. The music was repetitive so I turned it off. The tennis controls were wonky. I often couldn’t hold the boat paddle properly in two hands and then when I did it took a while before I started moving. 

As a 49 yo male I’m probably not the intended audience. My current favorite workout apps are Supernatural, Audio Trip, Pistol Whip, and the stamina training mode in Knockout League.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback! We're sorry to hear the guide's voice and the repetitive elements didn’t work for you. We understand that everyone has different preferences, and we appreciate your insights.

We recommend turning on the auto-grab feature, which might make the gameplay feel smoother and help with the paddle mechanics. We’ll also continue working on improving those elements.

We’ll keep your feedback in mind for future updates. Thanks again for sharing your experience!

reviews.review_description: On more than one occasion the daily challenge just doesn’t respond or work. I am at level 8 boss fight in the game. Made it to the last level and wasn’t able to grab hold of the exercise item to play.

reviews.review_title: The best fitness experience ive ever had
reviews.review_description: Okay I just bought the game and tried it out and ITS SO GOOD. The mixed reality works perfectly for the space I have - I can see my wife on the couch laughing at me flapping like a bird and I have a clear view of the game - this is both a super fun VR variety game while also being an intense work out. I played the tutorial, then setup my profile, then played the first level and then fought the boss and I am drenched in sweat and had to stop. I played this with a BOBO M3 PRO halo-style head mount with the facial interface off to prevent make face getting hot and it was perfectly comfortable. The game is just filled to the brim with different exercises to create movement or shoot at a boss - the game is tracking your head movement and your hand movement to ensure you're doing the correct movements, so you gotta keep moving no slacking or your power bar goes down and you'll move slowly or lose health etc. It mixes it up - like doing quick sets at the gym - like one moment I'm rowing, short break, then skiing, short break, then flapping my arms like a bird, then hitting projectiles with a racket, then punching cubes flying at me - its like they thought of every single thing thats fun to do in VR that requires you to move - I just hope I can keep at it and progress through the levels and it doesn't get repetitive ( but judging by the trailer theres a LOT more different exercises to come). This is how you gamify exercise - I mean theres plenty of VR games where you work up a sweat but the variety and the caleries burnt and the way it pushes you makes this so good. The graphics are nice - this type of cartoony art style works very well for VR - there seems to be some story about restoring the lands and harnessing magic but you dont need to pay attention to it - oh and no subscription just a once off purchase - bargain! Get it for your health, plus its a great introduction type game to show friends what VR can do and laugh at them doing the exercises. 10/10

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the fantastic review! 🎉 We’re thrilled to hear you’re loving the experience and enjoying the variety of exercises! 💪 It’s awesome that you’re finding it both fun and intense—it’s exactly what we aimed for! We also love the mixed reality setup you’ve got going on with your BOBO M3 PRO. 😄

We’re excited that you’re looking forward to more levels and exercises! And we’re glad the game has been a great way to introduce VR to your friends too. 🙌 We appreciate the 10/10 rating and hope you continue having fun as you progress through the game! 🚀🔥

reviews.review_description:
It kicked my butt! And I loved it!
This game gives you direct, clear instructions, with video examples.
Definitely worth the money (which is surprisingly low priced.

Boxing and rope exercise doesn't work properly. I tried moving myself forward and backwards to help better connect with the targets, but it just doesn't work properly. Tennis also seems a bit off.  Playing on quest 3. Refunding since too many exercises aren't well implemented.

developer_response.body: We appreciate your feedback and are sorry to hear about your experience. 🥺 Boxing has a hit power detection system 🥊, and the rope exercises require precise hand movement to connect with objects. We recommend trying training mode for Tennis 🎾, as it can help with accuracy. That said, we understand your frustration and will continue working on improving the tracking and overall experience. Your input helps us refine the game, and we hope you’ll consider giving it another chance in the future! 🙌

reviews.review_description:
I bought this game 1 or 2 weeks after it was released, and I try to do a session every day or every other day.

I really enjoy the adventure style of the game, which takes us through different worlds and changing environments. I'm currently on the third world/planet.

Critique Points

General

Difficulty Level: There’s no information explaining what the different difficulty settings actually change in the game.

Adventure Mode: The “Challenge” points don’t seem to serve any real purpose and feel mostly optional.

Goal of the Game: I understand the goal is to encourage daily exercise through gameplay, but in terms of adventure, you don’t really feel any progression. Nothing really changes from World 1 to World 2 or 3, and so on.

Boss Fights: I’d love to see some of the exercises unique to the boss fights integrated into the rest of the game. I also don’t quite understand the point of the mini wave segments. What’s the purpose of knocking out the boss if you don’t attack during that phase and only have to defend?


Exercises

Rotating Shield: The grip feels really awkward… Probably one of the least popular exercises among players.

Boxing: Some of the movements don’t feel like boxing at all. For example, one move feels like I’m trying to uppercut someone who’s 6’6”, and another feels more like a hammer fist than a punch. It would be nice to add more boxing-like targets like low punches or body punches — try to combine them for a more authentic experience.

Tennis: The grip feels weird. It’s like a racket was glued into my hand rather than actually holding one.


My Wishlist

Customization: It would be great to use the purple orbs as currency. We could buy to change the color of our clothes and gear or base

Mini Games: Once the adventure mode is finished, there’s not much left aside from the Daily Challenge. It would be nice to have access to different workout programs based on the duration and type of cardio you want. For example, if I want to do 20 minutes of boxing.

Exercises: The fitness world is full of different exercises, so adding new ones would be very welcome.


Here’s my feedback — keep up the great work, you’re definitely on the right track

Soo just got the game and have played it about 3 times. 
I like the challenges and also the simplicity of the game. I would like to see Hand tracking in the game. I have a couple exercis ideas with hand tracking that might be able to integrate into the game. But so far so good👏

Not hating, just not my vibe. 

The environments are charming, but the story, the scripts, and the characters make me feel like I'm playing a kid's game. Maybe that was the point. And if it motivates you, awesome! You should bring your own music because I only heard one repetitive track and it wasn't great (remember, you can stream Spotify in the browser) . 

I'll keep playing Les Mills, Underdogs, and Thrill of the Fight! 

BTW, something's wrong with your squat workout. I'm 6'1" and I have to break form to make my hands touch the ground to score in the green zone. It's also unclear how to catch the birds, is it moving fast? Is it holding the squat longer? I couldn't find a consistent rhythm like I could with the other workouts.

developer_response.body:
We appreciate your honest feedback! 😊 We understand that the game's style may not be for everyone, but we’re glad you found the environments charming and got a good workout! 💪 Thanks for the music suggestion—streaming your own tracks is a great tip! 🎶

We also appreciate your notes on the squat workout. At 6'1", you shouldn't have to break form, so we’ll look into improving the height calibration. As for catching the birds, it’s a mix of speed and timing—we’ll work on making that clearer! 🐦

Thanks again for trying it out, and we hope you keep crushing your workouts! 🔥

reviews.review_description: I really enjoy this game.  I have read other reviews where people say it lacks accuracy. I don't see that at all. I have played all the courses up to 56 (something different after that I don't know). However, on accuracy, it seems very accurate with the tennis and boxing. It does require precise hits in boxing when they are moving fast. But that's what you want in a game. I wish they had an option for people with some limited mobility. I'm 70 years old and luckily can move around pretty good and do squats. I have friends that would love it, but can't do squats. As far as I see you can't progress without squats. It should be an easy software implementation to just have it not run the particular exercises for a selected limited mobility. Would open the game up to a lot more people

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback! We're excited to let you know that we are working on adding accessibility features in the next update to accommodate users with limited mobility. Your suggestion about skipping certain exercises like squats will definitely help make the game more inclusive! Stay tuned! 😊

reviews.review_description: This game is ok but not what I expected. I didn’t know it would be obvious exercise as you just move through some different scenes. It’s like Holofit but Holofit is better. I thought it would be more of physical adventure game that disguises the exercise more. I like some of the exercises but some do not work well. I get a lot of misses in boxing that are inexplicable. I don’t know how the game choses what exercises you do but sometimes it’s the same 1 or 2 over and over and you’re doing squats for 10 or 20 minutes. The boss fights are terrible. They last like 15 minutes. It takes forever and just drags. Super annoying. And then you fight the same boss more than once. I’d like to shut the subtitles off. The story basically says the same thing over and over and the tips during exercises tell you to do exactly what you are already doing.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback! 😊 We’re sorry the game didn’t meet your expectations. Our goal was to create an engaging fitness experience, but we understand that everyone has different preferences. 💪 We appreciate your insights on the exercises, boss fights, and subtitles—your comments help us improve. 🔧 We’ll take this into account as we continue refining the game. If you have any specific suggestions, we’d love to hear them! 🎮✨

reviews.review_title: So close to perfect, just needs a tweak.
reviews.review_description:
First, I had a great time playing this fitness game. The story is enough to gel everything together without being too grating.  I also found the exercises never outstayed their welcome, nor did they get too repetitive. That's a big plus for me because even though I LOVE les mils and beat saber, it can get samey sometimes. 

BUT!  Why the 4 stars? 

The tennis is kind of bad, almost unplayable for me. I own 2 other VR tennis games, so I know what tennis should feel like. While you're working out, and pushing yourself, tennis pops up. I was thinking awesome, this will be fun, and it will lock on aim when needed or have the physics like the other tennis games you might expect, nope. You need to not only hit the ball, with force, while possibly tired, but you ALSO have to account for the aim, direction, and  precision of your hit. I found this was a big ask. The ball hitting mechanic itself is very clunky and disconnected. Furthering the problems, to hit the left and right enemies, it seems like the developers  want you to shift between forehand and then backhand, but it's asking a lot. The angle of the racket never moves, so you have to shift and turn your entire body to get the aim right.  It gets very clunky, unresponsive, and leads to a lot of frustration. On the first boss, when tennis came up, it was throwing things at me and I had to aim precisely with the tennis ball to hit these things. After missing repeatedly, I just took the hits and gave up until the next movement popped up.

SUGGESTIONS:  
1: Update the tennis with a lock on, so that when I hit the ball goes to the appropriate/most immediate/important target. 
2:Improve the physics so the ball is more accurate.
3: Allow me to completely remove tennis from the game by adding an option to select what exercises I don't or do want in the game.  

I just want to focus on hitting the ball with power and it doing the rest for me so I can enjoy the workout - not actually learn to play tennis and be accurate.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback! We're glad you're enjoying the game and its variety. 😊

We hear you on the tennis mechanics and understand the frustration. Improving responsiveness and making it feel smoother is something we’ll look into for future updates. Your suggestion for a lock-on or better physics is super helpful!

Thanks for your support, and we appreciate your input! 🚀🎾

reviews.review_description: Wow! I'm pleasantly surprised! This cartoon game has powerful packed workouts! My heart rate tops at 170-180!  I am a Les Mills dedicated fan since day One- when the app hit the store.  However, using this app works me out just as intense! The movements in the workouts are different than Les Mills, which gives my joints variability.  I am able to maintain the intensity that I want working out without over stressing my joints.

developer_response.body: Wow, thank you so much for the amazing review! 😊 We’re thrilled to hear that you’re loving the intensity of the workouts and that it’s giving you such a great heart rate boost! 💪 It’s awesome that the game provides the intensity you want without stressing your joints—variety is key, and we’re so happy it’s working well for you. Thanks for the comparison to Les Mills, that’s such a huge compliment! Keep up the fantastic work, and we hope you continue enjoying the game and getting great results! 🚀🎮

reviews.review_description:
Unfortunately tennis requires far too much accuracy and results in an uneven workout between your arms as you can't change racket hand. Would be better to just have rackets in both arms and give the option - something more like a supernatural type swing than actual tennis.

Otherwise this is great.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback! 😊 We’re really glad to hear you’re enjoying the game overall! We totally get what you mean about tennis—it can definitely be tough with the precision required. Your suggestion to have rackets in both hands is a great one, and we’ll definitely look into offering a more natural swing, like in Supernatural. 🎾💪 Thanks for helping us improve the game, and we hope you continue having a great time! 😄

reviews.review_description:
I was on the fence about this and the other newly released fitness game. I went for this one since it looked like the other one would get boring, Well.. this does too eventually. The exercises are very well designed and executed, but when it's all not new anymore you're basically just tiring yourself and looking at things happening that are (mostly) out of your control or moving because of your actions. And that's about it. It's a fitness app with a bit of a game-like disguise, instead of a game with a fitness-disguise. That's what I was hoping it would have been.

It looks pretty good graphically, could get some polish here and there but it's more than ok for the purpose.

I use my quest mainly as a way to exercise on my own.  What I like about this is being matched to folks who have similar physical preferences. I'm 54 and scheduled for a hip replacement in a couple months so I set mine to Easy. I was surprised at how fun it was competing against similar strangers, it genuinely stimulated me to work harder.  That being said...what is awkward about it is the size of the props I guess..I'm 5'3 so I prefer adjusting height and arm span.  So...maybe you can add Modified Exercises. The game itself is a lot of fun and you really feel the work. I usually do Supernatural on High so I Know I can do this game, just need some more options or fine tuned control. Another example for height is the tennis portion, I can't reach the overhead shots.  Anyway I recommend for everyone and implore the Devs for tweakage!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your thoughtful review! 🙌 We’re really glad to hear that the game has been motivating and fun for you, especially while working at your own pace. 💪 We appreciate your suggestion about adding more options for height and arm span adjustments—it’s a great idea, and we’ll definitely pass it along to the team. 🎾 Your feedback on the tennis portion and the need for more control options is also really valuable.

We’ll be updating the game really soon, so keep an eye out for those improvements! 🚀 We hope you continue enjoying the game, and we’ll keep working on ways to make it even better for all players. Thanks again for the recommendation and support! 😊😊

reviews.review_description: Excellent exercise, brilliant gameplay, but oh dear, that little blue puff of smoke and her irritating voice, and the annoying cackles of the fantasy falcons dropped it from five stars to three.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your review and for the kind words about the gameplay and exercise! 😊 We're really glad to hear you're enjoying the game overall. We also totally understand your frustration with the little blue puff of smoke and the fantasy falcons. 🎮💨 We’re always looking for ways to improve the user experience, so your feedback is definitely valuable.

We’ll pass along your thoughts to the team, and we hope to make the game even more enjoyable in future updates. Thanks again for sharing your thoughts with us! 🙏 Keep having fun, and we appreciate your support! 💪

reviews.review_description: As the title says overall fun and not too bad of and exercise,feels as the story part might become annoying as seems level sort of repeat themselves. The hit box needs improvement especially the boxing part but tennis and rope also suffer from it.

developer_response.body: Thanks for sharing your feedback! 😊 We’re happy to hear you're having fun and getting a good workout. 💪 We agree that the story could use some freshening up, and we’re working on keeping things engaging for longer play sessions. As for the hitbox issue, we're aware of it, and updates are in progress to improve that, including for boxing, tennis, and rope exercises. Stay tuned for improvements, and thanks for your support! 🙏✨

reviews.review_title: Pretty good fun for gamified workouts
reviews.review_description:
I do love this. 
les Mills boxing is my go to and by far the best fitness app on the store but i can never take it easy with that one and it's always giving 110 percent effort. 

Fitness fables is an great app to play for some dynamic cardio after tour regular workout. Let's say after a session of weight training or on your rest days. That's not to diminish it as a fitness app neither is it to say that if this is the only exercise someone is getting them this is a fantastic option. No micro transactions. No subscriptions and  cute little fantasy mini games to motivate those more adverse to regular fitness. 

I will criticize though and say the movements do feel very samey and limited after just a short amount of time and also the stupid sprite voice every time you do something is beyond irritating.  Would love that thing to be an accessibility option you can turn off. 

I will add this into my workout schedule for sure. Not how long it will stay fresh for though.

I was about to give this game a 5, but the squat section just doesn’t work. Yes I’m doing them properly, im in the right area, I reset the standing position. I’m not new to VR. 

There is nothing more frustrating than a mechanics that breaks the game. The game finally just pushed me forward to the next exercise. I couldn’t even cheat it, so I could just move forward. It just ruined the exercise. 

The tennis needs some tweaking, but at least it’s playable. 

Other than that I LOVE the game, and really hope they fix this issue. The skiing and other squats games works fine.. so I know it’s just that one area.

developer_response.body:
Hey tekkenfighter123, thanks for your detailed feedback! In the Defense section, there are actually two different squat mechanics:

1️⃣ One where you squat and hold to create a shield.
2️⃣ Another where you need to continuously squat to generate the shield.

If the mechanic isn’t registering correctly, try adjusting your stance and ensuring tracking is stable. We’re always working on improvements, so we appreciate your input! Glad to hear you’re enjoying the game overall—we’ll take a look at the tennis mechanics too. 🎾💪

reviews.review_description: I'm not really a fan of these types of games—they've always seemed a bit unserious and boring to me. But in this case, I can genuinely say that I'm impressed. There are so many great exercises and interactive elements that you could easily play for hours. But you won’t—because you’ll be exhausted! This game really makes you work up a sweat. I don’t regret a single cent I spent on it.

developer_response.body: Wow, thank you so much for the kind words! 😊 We're thrilled to hear that we’ve surprised you in the best way possible! 💪 It’s awesome that you’re enjoying the variety of exercises and interactive elements. The fact that you’re working up a sweat is exactly what we hoped for! 😅 Your support means a lot to us, and we’re so glad you feel the game is worth every cent. Keep up the great work, and we hope you continue having fun and getting fit! 🚀🎮

reviews.review_description:
A great VR fitness game that has made my workouts both effective and fun! It’s not just exercise—it’s a real adventure where you move, dodge, punch, and complete various challenges.
After just a week, I could feel my arms and legs getting stronger, and most importantly—I actually started losing weight! The gameplay is so engaging that workouts fly by unnoticed, yet they still make you break a sweat. Plus, it’s a great way to relieve stress after a hard day.
If you find the gym boring, I highly recommend giving this a try! It’s fun and you never feel like you’re just forcing yourself to work out.

developer_response.body:
hank you so much for the incredible review! 🙌 We’re thrilled to hear that the game has made your workouts both fun and effective! 💪 It’s awesome that you're seeing great results, like stronger arms and legs, and even weight loss after just a week! 🎉 The fact that you're enjoying it as an adventure while also breaking a sweat is exactly what we hoped for. We’re so glad it’s been a great stress reliever for you too.

Your recommendation means a lot to us, and we hope you continue enjoying the game and your fitness journey! 🚀 Keep up the amazing work! 😄🎮

reviews.review_title: Good fitness game, most bugs fixed
reviews.review_description:
I have 15 of the most popular fitness apps and this is very good.  Work up a really good sweat and it works out your whole body.  That said, I have had troubles with it telling me I was changing to the curls and just kept playing the drums.  Finally used the meta button to exit the game, resumed, and it took me to a totally different exercise.  Also found the tennis quite often switches to the tiny racket even I am holding the trigger.
Decided to update this review to 5 stars as I've been using it daily for about a month now.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your thoughtful review! 😊 We're so glad to hear you're enjoying the game and getting a great workout! 💪 It sounds like you've run into a couple of bugs, though, and we really appreciate you bringing them to our attention. Just to clarify, the drums exercise requires a specific power or intensity level to switch to the next movement, which might explain why it got stuck. As for the tennis racket issue, we're definitely looking into that and will work to fix it in upcoming updates. If you have any more details or need further assistance, feel free to reach out to us at fitnessfables.support@immersion.pl. Thanks for being part of the community, and we’re excited to continue improving your experience! 🚀🎮

reviews.review_description:
I really wanted a VR exercise game like Ringfit Adventures and this was it! I enjoyed the variety of the exercises and the intensity, though I think I do miss the light jogs from Ringfit and Shardfall. 

Some issues/questions I ran into after playing for an hour:
1) I think some of the games are either not tracking hands well or just hard to play - namely battle tennis (aiming is very hard), and left/right rows.
2) The first time I got to climbing ropes/bars I almost had to quit because I kept falling - there were no previews and for someone who doesn't play many VR games its not as intuitive.
3) Boss fights feel that it prioritizes speed which makes some forms much stronger than others. There should be some consideration for resistance like holding a squat longer (I guess it's harder to do this than Ringfit without a resistance ring to push/pull which affects intensity).
4) I'm not sure if game difficulty affects leaderboards?
5) I can't find an "Exit game" button on the main page.

With daily training and 80 stages I can see myself playing this for years!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your thoughtful feedback! 😊 We're thrilled to hear you're enjoying the variety and intensity—especially as a Ringfit fan! 🎮💪

We appreciate your insights on tracking, climbing mechanics, and boss fights. Your points about difficulty settings and leaderboards are great questions, and we’ll be sure to pass your feedback along to the team. As for exiting the game, we’ll look into making that more intuitive!

If you ever need assistance or have more suggestions, feel free to reach out to fitnessfables.support@immersion.pl. Enjoy your workouts, and we hope you keep having fun for years to come! 🚀🔥

reviews.review_title: Best fitness game I have ever played.
reviews.review_description: This is such a great looking game with real workouts. The scenery puts you in a good mood to play and work out. Some of the exercises take a little practice to get right, but once you figure it out, it works well. I could barely move my arms and legs the first time I played, and that's fantastic. I have incorporated this in my weekly cardio routine. I would give this a higher score if it was possible. I am looking forward to where the developers can take this in the future.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the incredible review! 🙌 We're thrilled to hear that the game is now part of your weekly routine and that it's giving you such a great workout! 💪 It's awesome that the scenery helps keep you motivated and in a good mood while working out. 🌟 We’re also glad to hear that you’re mastering the exercises over time!

Your support and excitement for future updates mean a lot to us, and we can’t wait to keep improving and adding more content. Thanks again for the glowing feedback—it really inspires us to keep pushing forward! 🚀🔥

reviews.review_description: I love this game. I am 60 and this helps me to stay active! Will you ever come out with multiplayer mode?
I would rate it 5 but it makes me really tired

Great game for my fitness really enjoying it but for one thing I set up my dominant hand wrong and can’t seem to change it in setup or reset the game I will give it a 4 star would of been 5 if I could of made changes or reset

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback! 😊 We’re so glad to hear you’re enjoying the game and that it’s helping with your fitness! 💪 We totally understand the frustration with setting up your dominant hand. While you can’t reset it in the settings right now, we’ll pass along your suggestion for adding an option to change it more easily. We really appreciate your understanding and hope this doesn’t stop you from fully enjoying the game. If you need any assistance, feel free to reach out to us at fitnessfables.support@immersion.pl. Thanks for your support, and we’re excited to keep improving the game! 🚀🎮
